title: Ipay International - Business Page
description: I developed a modern, responsive corporate landing platform for iPay International using Next.js, React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS, featuring a modular multi-section design, theme support, and reusable components for scalability and maintainability. The platform delivers a polished user experience across devices and was successfully deployed using Vercel.

title: UI/UX Landing Page
description: Developed the UI/UX for our midterm project landing page using Next.js, ensuring a fully responsive design across all devices. Implemented dark and light mode themes for improved user experience and deployed the project on Vercel.

title: Daily Monitoring Page - X-Meta System
description: Developed a Daily Monitoring Dashboard that tracks real-time device play data with date and device-type filtering, dynamic AJAX data fetching, and interactive UI components. The system includes summarized metrics, detailed data tables, and optimized loading, error, and empty states, ensuring a responsive, user-friendly, and maintainable monitoring solution.

title: Users Management Page - X-Meta System
description: I developed a User Management System that allows administrators to manage users, roles, access permissions, and Program ID assignments within the platform. The system includes role-based access control, ensuring only authorized users (e.g., admin) can perform create, edit, and delete actions, while others operate in read-only mode. I implemented dynamic data handling with database integration, including user status synchronization, program ID mapping with external data sources, and formatted access date ranges. The interface features a structured data table with dropdown handling for multiple program IDs, real-time statistics (total, active, inactive users), and clear UI states for system feedback. Additionally, the system is designed to be scalable and maintainable, with modular helper functions and safeguards for missing data or database dependencies.

title: Job Application Tracker
description: A full-stack web application I personally built to help job seekers stay on top of every application throughout the entire hiring process. The system features secure user authentication using JWT, allowing users to manage their job applications with full add, edit, and delete capabilities — tracking key details like company, role, status, date, notes, and resume file uploads. A four-stage status pipeline — Applied, Interview, Offer, and Rejected — keeps progress clear and organized, while a live dashboard delivers real-time statistics and a visual chart of the overall application breakdown. The project was designed and developed entirely from scratch, spanning database architecture, RESTful API development, React frontend engineering, and a cohesive custom dark UI design system.

title: Online Saving Goal system for Working Students
description: I developed an Online Saving Goal System for Working Students as my college capstone project, helping users create, track, and manage savings goals with real-time calculations and data analytics. The system features admin and user dashboards, goal locking, smart withdrawal redistribution, OTP email verification, forgot/reset password via PHPMailer, and protected routes using React Router DOM and Context. Built with React.js, Tailwind CSS, PHP, and MySQL, it uses Axios for data fetching, includes a global alert system, and is fully responsive across all devices.

title: Coffee Shop Website
description: I developed a personal coffee shop website using HTML, Tailwind CSS v4 with DaisyUI, and React.js v19 to create a clean, modern, and fully responsive user interface. The design was styled using Tailwind CSS and DaisyUI components for a polished, consistent layout. React.js powered the dynamic functionality, including an “Add to Basket” feature that allows users to select and manage products in real-time. I implemented persistent state management by integrating localStorage, enabling the shopping basket contents to be saved and restored across page reloads or sessions. Product data was fetched from a local JSON file using useEffect with async/await, and I incorporated try-catch-finally blocks to gracefully handle loading states and potential errors, enhancing the user experience with a loading spinner during data retrieval. The site adapts smoothly across all screen sizes, ensuring a seamless experience on both desktop and mobile devices. I deployed the project using Vercel, making it accessible online and production-ready. This project strengthened my front-end development skills in component structure, state management, data persistence, data fetching, and responsive design.

title: Food Recipes Website
description: I developed a Personal Food Recipes Website using React JS v19, Tailwind CSS v4, Daisy UI, and React Router. The website is fully responsive, ensuring an optimal viewing experience across all devices. It features advanced functionalities such as search filtering and category filtering, allowing users to easily find recipes based on their preferences. I have implemented data fetching using useEffect, alongside async/await for asynchronous operations, and included try/catch/finally for error handling to ensure smooth execution. The recipe data is stored in a JSON file, making it easy to manage and update. Additionally, I utilized React Router and useParams to dynamically navigate to specific recipe details based on the recipe ID in the URL. Once the project was completed, I deployed it on Vercel for live access and pushed the code to GitHub for version control and collaboration. This project showcases my skills in modern web development and creating user-friendly interfaces with dynamic content.

title: Amazon Website Clone
description: I developed an Amazon website clone as a personal project using HTML, Tailwind CSS v4, and Vanilla JavaScript to replicate the design, layout, and core functionality of the original site without any frameworks. Product data is fetched from a local JSON file to dynamically render product listings, and I implemented key features such as adding items to the cart, updating quantities, deleting items, and saving the cart state using localStorage for persistence. I also created a real-time search function to filter products based on user input. The website is fully responsive, adapting seamlessly across various screen sizes from mobile to desktop. This project allowed me to sharpen my front-end development skills by focusing on clean code structure, efficient DOM manipulation, and delivering a smooth, user-friendly shopping experience. Once completed, I deployed the website on Vercel for live access and pushed the code to GitHub for version control and collaboration.

title: Nike Website Clone
description: I developed a Nike website clone using HTML, Tailwind CSS, DaisyUI, and Vanilla JavaScript to create a modern, responsive user interface. Tailwind CSS and DaisyUI were used to craft a clean and visually appealing layout, with DaisyUI components enhancing the UI for a more polished and consistent design. Vanilla JavaScript handled the dynamic functionality, including product display, cart management, and real-time updates to the interface. Product data is stored in a JSON file and fetched on the front end to render product details dynamically and manage the shopping cart experience. The site offers interactive features such as adding or removing items from the cart, viewing detailed product information, and smooth navigation throughout the interface. This project allowed me to deepen my front-end development skills by focusing on DOM manipulation, data handling, and building a fully responsive and engaging shopping experience without relying on any frameworks. Once completed, I pushed the project to GitHub for version control and collaboration.

title: React Jobs Website
description: I developed a React-based jobs website using React.js v19 and Tailwind CSS v4, creating a responsive and user-friendly platform for job listings. Tailwind CSS was used to design a clean and modern layout, ensuring the website adapts seamlessly to all devices, from desktops to smartphones. I used JSON Server to serve the job data, allowing dynamic fetching and rendering of job listings on the front end. React Router was implemented to enable smooth navigation between different pages, providing an intuitive browsing experience. This project strengthened my skills in component-based development, state management, and client-side routing. Once completed, I uploaded the project to GitHub for version control and collaboration.

title: Movies Website API
description: I developed a dynamic movie website using React.js that fetches movie data from an external API and displays it on the front end. The project includes a fully responsive design, ensuring seamless viewing across all devices. I used React.js not only for designing and layout but also to implement key functionalities, such as a debounced search feature for smooth and efficient searching. The website efficiently handles API data, providing users with real-time movie information and an intuitive user experience. This project showcases my ability to combine front-end design with functional logic, delivering a professional and responsive web application. Once completed, I pushed the project to GitHub for version control and collaboration.

title: Zustand Habit-List React
description: I developed a Habit List app using Zustand for state management in React. The app allows users to add habits with a frequency of either daily or weekly, and the data is stored persistently in local storage using Zustand's persist middleware, ensuring the habit list is saved even after page reloads. I use Zustand's set and get methods for all functions, including adding new habits, marking habits as complete, and deleting them. The get method retrieves the current state, while set updates the state based on user actions, ensuring the app stays up to date. In addition to managing the habit list, the app tracks statistics such as the total number of habits, the count of habits completed today, and the longest streak of completed habits along with their names. These statistics are also managed within the set and get methods in the store.js file, allowing for efficient state updates and accurate progress tracking. I used MUI (Material-UI) for several components, including progress components, to visually represent habit completion and streaks. This enhances the user experience with a clean and responsive design. Once the app was developed, I pushed the code to GitHub and deployed it on Vercel for live access, ensuring seamless integration between development and production environments.

title: Simple Todo-List React JS
description: I developed a simple To-Do List application using Vanilla JavaScript for functionality and Tailwind CSS for styling and design. The app allows users to add, edit, and delete tasks, with all data being saved in localStorage to preserve tasks even after refreshing the page. The use of Tailwind CSS ensures a clean and modern design, while Vanilla JavaScript efficiently manages the state, user interactions, and local storage operations. This project showcases my ability to build functional and visually appealing applications using modern front-end technologies with persistent data handling.

title: Simple Rock Paper Scissor Game
description: I developed a Simple Rock Paper Scissors Game using HTML for the structure, pure CSS for clean and simple designs, and Vanilla JavaScript for functionality. The game allows players to choose their moves, while the computer generates a random move. The scores and game records are saved in local storage, allowing users to track their progress even after refreshing the page. I also implemented an auto-play feature that can be toggled by pressing 'A' to start and 'P' to stop, or by clicking a button. This project demonstrates my ability to create interactive and responsive web applications using core front-end technologies.

title: GYM Website UI and Functionality
description: I developed the UI for our System Analysis and Design project during my 3rd year, 1st semester as a Bachelor of Science in Information Systems (BSIS) student. I used pure HTML for structuring and CSS for styling and layout, ensuring the design is fully responsive across all devices through CSS media queries. The interface is clean and user-friendly, providing a consistent and smooth experience on desktops, tablets, and mobile devices. Additionally, I implemented functional buttons and modals using JavaScript, enhancing user interaction and improving the overall user experience. This project reflects my ability to create responsive, interactive, and well-organized layouts using core front-end technologies.

title: Instagram Clone UI
description: I created an Instagram clone using HTML for structuring the layout and pure CSS for styling and design. The project replicates the core design and user interface of Instagram, including the feed layout, profile section, and navigation bar. It is fully responsive, ensuring a consistent and smooth experience across all devices, from desktops to mobile phones. This project demonstrates my ability to create clean, modern designs and responsive layouts using only HTML and CSS, highlighting my front-end development skills.

title: Facebook Login Clone UI
description: I developed a Facebook Login UI Clone using HTML for the structure and pure CSS for styling and design. The clone replicates the look and feel of the official Facebook login page, including the login form, branding, and overall layout. The design is fully responsive across all devices, achieved through CSS media queries to ensure a consistent and smooth user experience on desktops, tablets, and mobile phones. This project demonstrates my ability to create responsive and visually accurate web interfaces using only HTML and CSS. Once completed, I pushed the project to GitHub for version control and collaboration and deployed it on Vercel for live access.
